revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11638480]] Wikidata name: Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omi no Kuni Fudoki''' (近江国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of [[:en:Omi Province|Omi Province]]. As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo legend]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|qq=|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]] (formerly [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]]) and the traditions of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of [[:en:Mount Ibuki|Ibuki Mountain]] and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].

Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo legend]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]] (formerly [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]]) and the traditions of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].

As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo legend]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]] (formerly [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]]) and the traditions of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of [[:en:Mount Ibuki|Ibuki Mountain]] and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11638480]] Wikidata name: Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omi no Kuni Fudoki''' (近江国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of [[:en:Omi Province|Omi Province]]. As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo legend]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]] (formerly [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]]) and the traditions of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of [[:en:Mount Ibuki|Ibuki Mountain]] and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11638480]] Wikidata name: Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omi no Kuni Fudoki''' (近江国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of [[:en:Omi Province|Omi Province]]. As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo legend]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]] (formerly [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]]) and the traditions of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of [[:en:Mount Ibuki|Ibuki Mountain]] and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11638480]] Wikidata name: Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omi no Kuni Fudoki''' (近江国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of [[:en:Omi Province|Omi Province]]. As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo legend]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]] (formerly [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]]) and the traditions of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of [[:en:Mount Ibuki|Ibuki Mountain]] and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].

Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo legend]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]] (formerly [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]]) and the traditions of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. == Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}

=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of [[:en:Mount Ibuki|Ibuki Mountain]] and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].

revision.comment: Add page with conflicting Wikidata QIDs
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11638480]] Wikidata name: Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omi no Kuni Fudoki''' (近江国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of [[:en:Omi Province|Omi Province]]. As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo legend]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]] (formerly [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]]) and the traditions of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of [[:en:Mount Ibuki|Ibuki Mountain]] and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].

== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}

This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11638480]] Wikidata name: Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omi no Kuni Fudoki''' (近江国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of [[:en:Omi Province|Omi Province]]. As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo legend]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]] (formerly [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]]) and the traditions of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of [[:en:Mount Ibuki|Ibuki Mountain]] and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].

Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11638480]] Wikidata name: Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omi no Kuni Fudoki''' (近江国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of [[:en:Omi Province|Omi Province]]. As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo legend]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]] (formerly [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]]) and the traditions of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of [[:en:Mount Ibuki|Ibuki Mountain]] and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related articles == * [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]] * [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo Legend]] * {{ill|Ika Clan|ja|伊香具神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika Clan|1=Ikagu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381016}}
{{translated page|ja|近江国風土記|version=101341371|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:近江国風土記]]

The most noteworthy fragments mention the [[:en:Swan maiden|Hagoromo legend]] associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in [[:en:Nagahama, Shiga|Nagahama]] (formerly [[:en:Ika District, Shiga|Ika District]]) and the traditions of [[:en:Chikubu Island|Chikubu Island]].
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>

=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|WD=Q2624360|lt=Ibuki Mountain}} and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|Fudoki|en|Fudoki|WD=Q1194534|lt=Fudoki}} * {{ill|Swan maiden|en|Swan maiden|WD=Q1326754|lt=Hagoromo Legend}} * {{ill|Ika Clan|ja|伊香具神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika Clan|1=Ikagu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381016}}

{{wikidata link|Q11638480}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Fudoki]] [[Category:History of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Lost books]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11638480]] Wikidata name: Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omi no Kuni Fudoki''' (近江国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of [[:en:Omi Province|Omi Province]]. As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the {{ill|Swan maiden|en|Swan maiden|WD=Q1326754|lt=Hagoromo legend}} associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama}} (formerly {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|en|Ika District, Shiga|WD=Q1042353|lt=Ika District}}) and the traditions of {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island}}.
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|WD=Q2624360|lt=Ibuki Mountain}} and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|Fudoki|en|Fudoki|WD=Q1194534|lt=Fudoki}} * {{ill|Swan maiden|en|Swan maiden|WD=Q1326754|lt=Hagoromo Legend}} * {{ill|Ika Clan|ja|伊香具神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika Clan|1=Ikagu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381016}}
{{translated page|ja|近江国風土記|version=101341371|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the {{ill|Swan maiden|en|Swan maiden|WD=Q1326754|lt=Hagoromo legend}} associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama}} (formerly {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|en|Ika District, Shiga|WD=Q1042353|lt=Ika District}}) and the traditions of {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island}}.
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}

'''Omi no Kuni Fudoki''' (近江国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of [[:en:Omi Province|Omi Province]]. As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the {{ill|Swan maiden|en|Swan maiden|WD=Q1326754|lt=Hagoromo legend}} associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama}} (formerly {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|en|Ika District, Shiga|WD=Q1042353|lt=Ika District}}) and the traditions of {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island}}.

=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|WD=Q2624360|lt=Ibuki Mountain}} and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近江国風土記]][[zh:近江國風土記]]
'''Omi no Kuni Fudoki''' (近江国風土記) is the [[Fudoki]] of [[:en:Omi Province|Omi Province]]. As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the {{ill|Swan maiden|en|Swan maiden|WD=Q1326754|lt=Hagoromo legend}} associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama}} (formerly {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|en|Ika District, Shiga|WD=Q1042353|lt=Ika District}}) and the traditions of {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island}}.
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Ikatsuomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|WD=Q2624360|lt=Ibuki Mountain}} and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Related articles == * {{ill|Fudoki|en|Fudoki|WD=Q1194534|lt=Fudoki}} * {{ill|Swan maiden|en|Swan maiden|WD=Q1326754|lt=Hagoromo Legend}} * {{ill|Ika Clan|ja|伊香具神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika Clan|1=Ikagu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381016}}
{{translated page|ja|近江国風土記|version=101341371|comment=Imported full ja history}}

As it exists only in the form of [[:en:Fragments|Fragments]], the content is known solely through [[:en:Secondary sources|Secondary sources]] such as the {{ill|Teio Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teio Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the {{ill|Swan maiden|en|Swan maiden|WD=Q1326754|lt=Hagoromo legend|1=Swan maiden}} associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama|1=Nagahama, Shiga}} (formerly {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|en|Ika District, Shiga|WD=Q1042353|lt=Ika District|1=Ika District, Shiga}}) and the traditions of {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island|1=Chikubu Island}}.
== Hagoromo Legend of Lake Yogo == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |古老傳曰 近江國伊香郡 與胡鄕 伊香小江 在鄕南也 天之八女 倶爲白鳥 自天而降 浴於江之南津 于時 伊香刀美 在於西山 遙見白鳥 其形奇異 因疑若是神人乎 往見之 實是神人也 於是 伊香刀美 卽生感愛 不得還去 竊遣白犬 盗取天羽衣 得隱弟衣 天女乃知 其兄七人 飛昇天上 其弟一人 不得飛去 天路永塞 卽爲地民 天女浴浦 今謂神浦是也 伊香刀美 與天女弟女 共爲室家 居於此處 遂生男女 男二女二 兄名意美志留 弟名那志登美 女伊是理比咩 次名奈是理比賣 此伊香連等之先祖是也 後 母卽捜天羽衣 着而昇天 伊香刀美 獨守空床 唫詠不斷 |attr1=Teio Henki, Yoro 7 (723)<ref name=Yamamoto>{{Cite book |author=Setsu Yamamoto |title=Myths of the Gods: Izanagi, Izanami, and the Celestial Maiden |publisher=Daiwakan Shoten |year=1989 |isbn=4-469-22066-3 |pages=505-511}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |古老の伝へて曰へらく、近江の国伊香郡与胡郷伊香小江、郷の南にあり。天の八女、倶に白鳥となりて、天より降りて江の南津に浴みき。時に伊香刀美、西山にありて白鳥を見るに、その形奇異なり。往きて見るに、実に神人なりき。伊香刀美、感愛を生じ、白犬を遣して天羽衣を盗み取らしむ。弟の衣を得て隠しき。天女、その兄七人は飛び昇るも、弟一人は飛び去らず。天路永く塞がれ、地民となる。天女の浴みし浦は神浦と呼ばれ、伊香刀美と天女は室家となり、二男二女を生む。兄は意美志留、弟は那志登美、姉は伊是理比咩、妹は奈是理比賣。これが伊香連の祖である。後に母は羽衣を得て天に昇る。伊香刀美は独り空床を守り、絶えず詠嘆した。 }}
=== Analysis === {{see also|Hagoromo Legend}}
This legend portrays {{ill|Ikatoomi|ja|伊香刀美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikatoomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊賀津臣命|1=Igatsuomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109599098}} as a man who encounters celestial maidens bathing in Lake Yogo. Identifying them as divine beings, he uses a white dog to steal the "celestial robe" (羽衣) of the youngest maiden, preventing her from returning to heaven. They marry and have two sons and two daughters, but the celestial maiden eventually regains her robe and ascends to heaven, leaving Ikatoomi behind.
The story, classified as a {{ill|Swan maiden tale|ja|Swan maiden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Swan maiden tale|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serves as a mythological origin for the {{ill|Ika clan|ja|伊香連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, enhancing their prestige.<ref name=bunkazai>{{Cite web|url=http://shiga-bunkazai.jp/ |title=Legend of the Celestial Maiden in Lake Yogo |publisher=Shiga Prefectural Cultural Heritage Association |date=2019}}</ref>
== Chikubu Island Tradition == {{Verse translation |head1=Original Text |lang1=ojp |又曰 霜速比古命之男 多々美比古命 是謂夷服岳神也 女比佐志比女命 是夷服岳神之姉 在於久惠峯也 次淺井比咩命 是夷服之姪 在淺井岡也 是 夷服岳與淺井丘 相競長高 淺井岡 一夜增高 夷服岳神 怒拔刀劍 殺淺井比賣 々々之頭 堕江中而成江島 名竹生島其頭乎 |attr1=Teio Henki, Gensho 7 (723)<ref name=Akimoto_459>{{Cite book |editor=Kichiro Akimoto |title=Fudoki |publisher=Iwanami Shoten |year=1993 |isbn=4-00-004487-7}}</ref> |head2=Modern Japanese |lang2=jp |霜速比古命の男、多々美比古命は夷服岳神。姉比佐志比女命は久恵峯に住まう。次に淺井比咩命、浅井の岡に住まう。夷服岳と浅井岡は長高を競い、浅井岡が一夜で高くなると、夷服岳神は怒り浅井比賣を殺す。彼女の頭が江中に堕ち、竹生島となる。 }}
=== Analysis === This tradition describes the violent mythological competition between the gods of {{ill|Mount Ibuki|en|Mount Ibuki|WD=Q2624360|lt=Ibuki Mountain|1=Mount Ibuki}} and the hill of {{ill|Asai|ja|浅井岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, leading to the creation of {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island|1=Chikubu Island}}.

Among the extant Fudoki fragments, it is the only one to use the phrase "According to the old elders (古老の伝へて曰ひしく)" in its narrative style.
The most noteworthy fragments mention the {{ill|Swan maiden|en|Swan maiden|WD=Q1326754|lt=Hagoromo legend|1=Swan maiden}} associated with {{ill|Lake Yogo|ja|余呉湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Yogo|1=Lake Yogo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385251}} in {{ill|Nagahama, Shiga|en|Nagahama, Shiga|WD=Q844130|lt=Nagahama|1=Nagahama, Shiga}} (formerly {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|en|Ika District, Shiga|WD=Q1042353|lt=Ika District|1=Ika District, Shiga}}) and the traditions of {{ill|Chikubu Island|en|Chikubu Island|WD=Q2975401|lt=Chikubu Island|1=Chikubu Island}}.
